title: The Bookbinder of Prague
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A Jewish bookbinder in 1941 Prague saves endangered manuscripts by binding them into the covers of banned Nazi propaganda.
long_description: In 1941 Prague, Jewish bookbinder David Rosen preserves forbidden Jewish texts and rare manuscripts by secretly binding them inside the covers of confiscated Nazi propaganda books. When SS officer Frau Doktor Greta Müller discovers discrepancies in the seized book inventory, she suspects someone in the state binding workshop. David's sixteen-year-old daughter Anna befriends the son of a Wehrmacht major, walking a dangerous line between survival and betrayal. As the net tightens and deportations begin, David must decide how many books — and how many lives — he can save before the Nazis uncover his secret.
main_characters: David Rosen — Jewish bookbinder, master craftsman, widower
Anna Rosen — David's daughter, 16, practical and brave
Frau Doktor Greta Müller — SS cultural officer overseeing confiscated books
Pavel Novak — David's apprentice, 19, impulsive and loyal
Rabbi Samuel Löw — Elder of the Jewish community
Major Klaus Weber — Wehrmacht officer, offers to help Anna escape
Erik Weber — Major Weber's son, 17, befriends Anna
